claims.text: Two infinite, perfectly conducting, parallel plates lie at z=0 and z=d.
The space between the plates is vacuum.
The tangential electric field vanishes on each plate: E_parallel = 0 at z=0,d.
claims.notes: Standard boundary condition for perfect conductors.

claims.text: The field is quantized in the region between the plates.
Edge effects are neglected because the plates are infinite.
Only the change in vacuum energy due to the plates is computed; absolute vacuum energy is divergent and unobservable.
The allowed electromagnetic modes are standing waves in the z-direction and plane waves in the transverse (x,y)-directions.
The allowed longitudinal wave numbers are k_z = nπ/d, n=0,1,2,...
The mode frequencies are ω_{n,k_⊥} = c√(k_⊥² + (nπ/d)²).
For n≥1, there are two polarizations (TE and TM).
For n=0, only one physical polarization contributes.
claims.notes: Actually n=0 TM mode is trivial; only TM gives E=0 identically.

claims.text: The zero-point energy per unit area is U(d)/A = (ℏ/2) ∫ d²k_⊥/(2π)² [ω_{0,k_⊥} + 2 Σ_{n=1}^∞ ω_{n,k_⊥}].
This zero-point energy expression is formally divergent.
An exponential cutoff e^{-ε ω} with ε>0 is introduced to suppress high-frequency modes.
The regulator will be removed by taking ε→0⁺ after subtraction of the free-vacuum contribution.
The regularized zero-point energy per unit area is U_ε(d)/A = (ℏc/2) ∫ d²k_⊥/(2π)² [k_⊥ e^{-εc k_⊥} + 2 Σ_{n=1}^∞ √(k_⊥²+(nπ/d)²) e^{-εc√(k_⊥²+(nπ/d)²)}].
The free-vacuum energy per unit area in a slab of width d is U^{(0)}_ε(d)/A = ℏ ∫ d²k_⊥/(2π)² (d/π) ∫_0^∞ dk_z c√(k_⊥²+k_z²) e^{-εc√(k_⊥²+k_z²)}.
The replacement rule for the continuum limit is Σ_{n=0}^∞' f(nπ/d) → (d/π) ∫_0^∞ dk_z f(k_z), where the prime means the n=0 term carries half weight.
The Casimir energy is U_{Cas,ε}(d)/A = U_ε(d)/A - U^{(0)}_ε(d)/A.
Define F(x) = ∫ d²k_⊥/(2π)² ℏc √(k_⊥²+x²) e^{-εc√(k_⊥²+x²)}.
U_ε(d)/A = Σ_{n=0}^∞' F(nπ/d), where the prime means half weight for n=0.
U_{Cas,ε}(d)/A = Σ_{n=0}^∞' F(an) - (1/a) ∫_0^∞ dx F(x), where a = π/d.
The Euler–Maclaurin formula gives Σ_{n=0}^∞' F(an) = (1/a) ∫_0^∞ F(x) dx - Σ_{m=1}^∞ [B_{2m}/(2m)!] a^{2m-1} F^{(2m-1)}(0).
After applying Euler–Maclaurin, U_{Cas,ε}(d)/A = -(a³/720) F^{(3)}(0) + ..., where omitted terms vanish or are canceled.
claims.notes: Model initially gets sign wrong, then corrects.

claims.text: The leading Euler–Maclaurin correction is -[B_4/4!] a³ F^{(3)}(0) = (a³/720) F^{(3)}(0).
Using polar coordinates, F(x) = (ℏc/2π) ∫_0^∞ k_⊥ dk_⊥ √(k_⊥²+x²) e^{-εc√(k_⊥²+x²)}.
Substituting u = √(k_⊥²+x²), so k_⊥ dk_⊥ = u du, gives F(x) = (ℏc/2π) ∫_x^∞ u² e^{-εcu} du.
As ε→0, ∫_x^∞ u² e^{-εcu} du = (divergent terms independent of x or polynomial in x) - x³/3 + O(ε).
claims.notes: Claim about the x-dependent finite piece.

claims.text: The x-dependent finite piece of F(x) is F(x) ~ -(ℏc/6π) x³ + (terms canceled by subtraction).
Substituting a = π/d gives U_{Cas}(d)/A = -(ℏc/(720π))(π/d)³ = -π²ℏc/(720 d³).
The Casimir energy per unit area is U(d)/A = -π²ℏc/(720 d³).
The force per unit area is F(d)/A = -d/d(d) [U_{Cas}(d)/A].
F(d)/A = -π²ℏc/(240 d⁴).
The negative sign of the force means the force is attractive.
An exponential cutoff e^{-εω} was used to regulate the formally divergent zero-point sums/integrals.
The subtraction of the free-vacuum contribution removes the divergent bulk vacuum energy, leaving a finite d-dependent remainder.
The Euler–Maclaurin formula was used to extract the finite difference between the discrete mode sum and the continuum integral.
No explicit zeta-function regularization was required in the main derivation.
A shorter alternative derivation uses the known analytically continued value ζ(-3) = 1/120.
ζ(-3) = 1/120 is obtained by analytic continuation of the Riemann zeta function, not from the convergent series Σ n³.
In free space there are 2 photon polarizations.
The density of states in k_z over an interval of length d is d/π for positive k_z.
The model initially writes U_{Cas,ε}(d)/A = -(a³/720)F^{(3)}(0) and then states 'the sign is clearly wrong'.
claims.notes: Model catches its own sign error and corrects it.

claims.text: After correcting the sign, U_{Cas}(d)/A = (a³/720) F^{(3)}(0).
The zero-point energy of each mode is ℏω/2.
The Euler–Maclaurin formula requires F to be sufficiently smooth and the regulator to be present.
In the limit ε→0⁺, the omitted Euler–Maclaurin terms vanish or correspond to divergent pieces canceled by subtraction.
